@graph.name: Siding Installation & Replacement - Capitol Improvements
@graph.description: James Hardie Preferred Partner installing fiber cement, vinyl, and engineered wood siding in Maryland, DC, and Virginia. Durable, low-maintenance siding with manufacturer-backed warranties.

itemOffered.name: James Hardie HardiePlank Lap Siding
itemOffered.description: The most popular fiber cement siding — durable, fire resistant, and ColorPlus pre-painted finish available.

itemOffered.name: James Hardie Shingle Siding
itemOffered.description: Fiber cement shingle-style siding for a cedar shake look without the maintenance.

itemOffered.name: James Hardie Panel Siding (HardiePanel)
itemOffered.description: Vertical panel fiber cement siding for modern or board-and-batten aesthetics.

itemOffered.name: Vinyl Siding Installation
itemOffered.description: Insulated and standard vinyl siding — low maintenance and available in a wide range of colors and profiles.

itemOffered.name: LP SmartSide Engineered Wood Siding
itemOffered.description: Engineered wood siding that looks like natural wood with improved durability and moisture resistance.

itemOffered.name: Cedar Shake & Shingle Siding
itemOffered.description: Natural and synthetic cedar shake siding installation for a classic New England aesthetic.

mainEntity.name: What is James Hardie fiber cement siding?

acceptedAnswer.text: James Hardie HardiePlank is a fiber cement siding made from cement, sand, and cellulose fiber. It resists fire, rot, insects, and moisture. As a James Hardie Preferred Partner, we can offer the full manufacturer warranty.

mainEntity.name: How long does siding installation take?

acceptedAnswer.text: A typical whole-home siding replacement takes 3–7 days depending on house size, existing material removal, and complexity.

mainEntity.name: Can you match my existing siding?

acceptedAnswer.text: In many cases yes, particularly with vinyl siding. For fiber cement, we can often match profile and paint color.
